name: Allan Arkush
description: Director of the 1979 cult classic Rock 'n' Roll High School and a prolific television director who logs new releases regularly on Letterboxd.

name: Aneesh Chaganty
description: Filmmaker behind the screen-based thrillers Searching and Run, and an active member of Letterboxd's director community.

name: Anthony Fantano
description: The internet's most-watched music critic, known as theneedledrop on YouTube, who also tracks his prolific film-watching on Letterboxd.

name: Brian Duffield
description: Screenwriter and director of Spontaneous and No One Will Save You, and a candid, frequent poster on Letterboxd.

name: Caddicarus
description: British YouTuber and video-game commentator Jim Caddick who also keeps an active film diary on Letterboxd.

name: Carrie Coon & Tracy Letts
description: The acclaimed actress of The Gilded Age and her Pulitzer-winning playwright husband share a single joint Letterboxd account.

name: Chad Hartigan
description: Indie writer-director of Morris from America and This Is Martin Bonner, and a consistent Letterboxd diarist.

name: Chandler Levack
description: Canadian filmmaker and film critic who directed the coming-of-age comedy I Like Movies and writes widely about cinema.

name: Charli XCX
description: Genre-bending pop musician behind Brat whose eclectic, art-house-leaning Letterboxd favorites are a fan fascination.

name: Chris Stuckmann
description: One of YouTube's biggest film reviewers who made his feature directorial debut with the horror film Shelby Oaks.

name: Christopher McQuarrie
description: Oscar-winning screenwriter of The Usual Suspects and director of the recent Mission: Impossible films.

name: CinemaStix
description: Reed Belk, whose CinemaStix channel makes sharp, concise video essays about film craft and technique.

name: Dacre Montgomery
description: Australian actor known for Stranger Things and Elvis who logs his watches on Letterboxd.

name: Dafne Keen
description: Actress known for Logan and His Dark Materials who reviews films on Letterboxd.

name: David Ehrlich
description: IndieWire's senior film critic, known for his elaborate annual video countdowns of the year's best films.

name: David F. Sandberg
description: Horror-turned-blockbuster director of Lights Out and the Shazam! films who posts on Letterboxd as 'ponysmasher.'

name: Demi Adejuyigbe
description: Comedian, writer, and podcaster celebrated for his elaborate September 21st videos, active on Letterboxd.

name: Eddy Burback
description: Comedy YouTuber known for his deadpan cultural deep-dives who tracks his films on Letterboxd.

name: Evan Puschak
description: Better known as the Nerdwriter, a video essayist whose analyses of film, art, and culture helped define the format.

name: Filmento
description: Film YouTuber known for dissecting what makes movie screenplays and blockbusters succeed or fail.

name: Francis Ford Coppola
description: Five-time Oscar-winning director of The Godfather and Apocalypse Now, present on Letterboxd as a patron member.

name: Hari Nef
description: Actress and model known for Barbie and Transparent, and an active Letterboxd reviewer.

name: Ian Hecox
description: Co-founder of the pioneering comedy channel Smosh who keeps a film diary on Letterboxd.

name: Jack Harlow
description: Grammy-nominated rapper and Hitchcock devotee whose Letterboxd favorites skew toward classic thrillers.

name: Jim Cummings
description: Actor-director of Thunder Road and The Wolf of Snow Hollow who champions independent film on Letterboxd.

name: Joe Lynch
description: Genre director of Mayhem and Everly, and an enthusiastic voice in the Letterboxd film community.

name: Joel Haver
description: Animator and independent filmmaker whose rotoscoped YouTube shorts and prolific output extend to a Letterboxd presence.

name: Joshua Rush
description: Actor known for Disney's Andi Mack who is an active Letterboxd cinephile.

name: Karsten Runquist
description: Video-essayist and film YouTuber whose reviews and personal filmmaking spill over onto Letterboxd.

name: Kyle Kallgren
description: Creator of Brows Held High, a video-essay series exploring arthouse, avant-garde, and experimental cinema.

name: Kyle MacLachlan
description: Actor synonymous with David Lynch's Twin Peaks and Blue Velvet who makes playful character-themed lists on Letterboxd.

name: Lukas Gage
description: Actor known for The White Lotus and Euphoria who reviews films on Letterboxd under a tongue-in-cheek handle.

name: Maggie Mae Fish
description: Actress and video essayist whose YouTube channel unpacks films and the politics beneath them.

name: Martin Scorsese
description: The legendary director of Taxi Driver and Goodfellas whose arrival made him one of Letterboxd's most-followed members.

name: Michael Mann
description: Director of Heat, Collateral, and Miami Vice whose Letterboxd favorites list drew wide attention.

name: Michael Mohan
description: Director of the horror hit Immaculate and the thriller The Voyeurs, active on Letterboxd.

name: Mike Flanagan
description: Horror auteur behind The Haunting of Hill House and Doctor Sleep, and a longtime Letterboxd member.

name: Nando v Movies
description: Matthew Kelly, whose Nando v Movies channel offers comedic deep dives into superhero and genre cinema.

name: Patrick H. Willems
description: Video essayist whose long-form YouTube films about cinema pair with an active Letterboxd account.

name: Paul Scheer
description: Comedian and host of the How Did This Get Made? podcast devoted to gloriously bad movies.

name: Rachel Sennott
description: Comedian and actress known for Shiva Baby and Bottoms, active on Letterboxd.

name: Rian Johnson
description: Writer-director of Knives Out and Brick whose sporadic, cryptic Letterboxd account is a puzzle for fans.

name: Ryan Hollinger
description: Horror-leaning video essayist whose YouTube channel analyzes genre films and why they resonate.

name: Sam Lerner
description: Actor known for Project Almanac and the animated Monster House, present on Letterboxd.

name: Schaffrillas
description: Creator of Schaffrillas Productions, whose comedic video essays rank and analyze animated films.

name: Scott Derrickson
description: Director of Sinister and Doctor Strange, and a prolific horror-loving Letterboxd member.

name: Sean Baker
description: Oscar-winning director of Anora and The Florida Project whose account is a treasure trove for independent-cinema fans.

name: Sean Chandler
description: Film YouTuber behind Sean Chandler Talks About Stuff, posting prolific movie reviews and rankings.

name: Spencer Agnew
description: Smosh host and comedy performer who logs his watches on Letterboxd.

name: Thomas Flight
description: Video essayist known for thoughtful, earnest analyses of film and visual storytelling on YouTube.

name: Wim Wenders
description: The German master behind Paris, Texas and Wings of Desire who consistently reviews films on Letterboxd.

name: Zachary Ruane
description: Comedian of the Australian sketch trio Aunty Donna, active on Letterboxd.

name: Ziwe
description: Comedian, writer, and provocateur behind the Showtime series Ziwe, active on Letterboxd.
